stem: An exterior angle of a triangle measures $140°$. The two non-adjacent interior angles are equal. What is the measure of each?
explanation: By the exterior angle theorem, the exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. If each is $x$, then $2x = 140°$, so $x = 70°$.

distractorRationale.A: Found the adjacent interior angle ($180° - 140° = 40°$) instead.
distractorRationale.D: Assumed each interior angle equals the exterior angle.
